subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(1-5)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below:
Eight persons sit in two rows that are parallel to each other where the persons A, B, C and D sit in Row 1 and face north while the persons P, Q, R and S sit in Row 2 and face south. Row 2 is in north of row 1.
All the information is not necessarily in the same order.
C doesn’t sit at any of the extreme ends. R sits second to the right of S. The one who faces S sits second to the right of D. A sits immediately to the right of D. Q doesn’t sit opposite to B.

Q 1. What is the position of B with respect to the person who sits opposite to Q?
q:
Directions $(1-5)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Eight persons sit in two rows that are parallel to each other where the persons A, B, C and D sit in Row1 and face north while the persons P, Q, R and S sit in Row2 and face south. Row2 is in north of row1.
All the information is not necessarily in the same order.
C doesn’t sit at any of the extreme ends. R sits second to the right of S. The one who faces S sits second to the right of D. A sits immediately to the right of D. Q doesn’t sit opposite to B.

Q1. What is the position of B with respect to the person who sits opposite to Q?
explanation: Row 2 (facing south): R(1), Q(2), S(3), P(4). Row 1 (facing north): D(1), A(2), C(3), B(4). The person opposite Q is A. B sits second to the right of A.

q:

Eight persons sit in two rows that are parallel to each other where the persons A, B, C and D sit in Row1 and face north while the persons P, Q, R and S sit in Row2 and face south. Row2 is in north of row1.

Q2. How many persons sit between the one who faces B and the one who sits immediately to the left of R?
explanation: The one facing B is P (pos 4). R is at pos 1 facing south, so immediately to the left of R is Q (pos 2). Only one person (S, pos 3) sits between P and Q.

options.text: The one who sits opposite to B

Q 5. If R is related to A and in the same way S is related to B, then who among the following is related to C?
q:
Directions $(1-5)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Eight persons sit in two rows that are parallel to each other where the persons A, B, C and D sit in Row1 and face north while the persons P, Q, R and S sit in Row2 and face south. Row2 is in north of row1.
All the information is not necessarily in the same order.
C doesn’t sit at any of the extreme ends. R sits second to the right of S. The one who faces S sits second to the right of D. A sits immediately to the right of D. Q doesn’t sit opposite to B.

Q5. If R is related to A and in the same way S is related to B, then who among the following is related to C?
explanation: The first person is immediately to the right of the one opposite to the second person. C is immediately to the right of A (who is opposite Q). Thus, Q is related to C.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(6-9)$: In each of the question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Give answer:

Q 6. Statements:
All Karate are Boxing.
No Judo are Wrestling.
Only a few Boxing are Wrestling.
Conclusions:
I. Some Karate can be Judo.
II. All Boxing can be Judo.
q:
Directions $(6-9)$: In each of the question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Give answer:

Q6. Statements:
All Karate are Boxing.
No Judo are Wrestling.
Only a few Boxing are Wrestling.
Conclusions:
I. Some Karate can be Judo.
II. All Boxing can be Judo.

options.text: Both conclusion I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Either conclusion I or II follows

explanation: I is possible (Some Karate being Judo does not violate any rule). II is false (since some Boxing are Wrestling, and no Judo is Wrestling, all Boxing can never be Judo). Only I follows.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(6-9)$: In each of the question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Give answer:

Q 7. Statements:
A few Parrots are Birds.
Only a few Birds are Peacock.
Some Ostrich are not Peacock.
Conclusions:
I. All Birds are Peacock.
II. Some Birds are not Peacock.
q:
Directions $(6-9)$: In each of the question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Give answer:

Q7. Statements:
A few Parrots are Birds.
Only a few Birds are Peacock.
Some Ostrich are not Peacock.
Conclusions:
I. All Birds are Peacock.
II. Some Birds are not Peacock.

options.text: Both conclusion I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Either conclusion I or II follows

explanation: Only a few Birds are Peacock implies some Birds are not Peacock (True), and all Birds cannot be Peacock (False). Only II follows.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(6-9)$: In each of the question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Give answer:

Q 8. Statements:
No Houses are White.
Only a few Brown are Houses.
Some Bricks are Brown.
Conclusions:
I. All Brown can be White.
II. Some Bricks are Houses is a possibility.
q:
Directions $(6-9)$: In each of the question below, some statements are given followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Give answer:

Q8. Statements:
No Houses are White.
Only a few Brown are Houses.
Some Bricks are Brown.
Conclusions:
I. All Brown can be White.
II. Some Bricks are Houses is a possibility.

options.text: Both conclusion I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Either conclusion I or II follows

explanation: I is false (the part of Brown that is Houses cannot be White). II is true (there is no restriction between Bricks and Houses). Only II follows.

Either conclusion I or II follows

explanation: I: Some Bold are definitely Underline (the part of Bold that is Italic), so stating it as a possibility is false. II: No relation is established between Straight and Italic. Neither follows.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: If in the given word “CHAMPION” we replace all the consonants with immediately preceding letter and all the vowels with immediately succeeding letter, then find how many vowels are there in the new arrangement?
q: If in the given word “CHAMPION” we replace all the consonants with immediately preceding letter and all the vowels with immediately succeeding letter, then find how many vowels are there in the new arrangement?
explanation: Replacing letters: C>B, H>G, A>B, M>L, P>O, I>J, O>P, N>M. In the new string 'BGBLOJPM', only 'O' is a vowel (one vowel).

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(11-13)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below:
A certain number of persons sit in a row and face north. R sits second the right of E and fourth from an extreme end. The number of persons sit between U and E is the same as the number of persons sit between Y and R. Five persons sit between Y and E. Z sits seventh to the left of Y. G sits fifth to the left of Y. Two persons sit between Y and M who sits second from one of the ends.

Q 11. What is the position of U with respect to M?
q:
Directions $(11-13)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
A certain number of persons sit in a row and face north. R sits second the right of E and fourth from an extreme end. The number of persons sit between U and E is the same as the number of persons sit between Y and R. Five persons sit between Y and E. Z sits seventh to the left of Y. G sits fifth to the left of Y. Two persons sit between Y and M who sits second from one of the ends.

Q11. What is the position of U with respect to M?
explanation: Seating layout (left to right): Z(1), E(2), G(3), R(4), _(5), U(6), _(7), Y(8), _(9), _(10), M(11), _(12). U is fifth to the left of M.

F 5 * E ^ 2 D U # Z R S & 8 9 @ J M 1 L O 3 X 7 % Q

q:
Directions $(14-18)$: Study the following alphanumeric symbol series carefully and answer the questions given below:
F 5 * E ^ 2 D U # Z R S & 8 9 @ J M 1 L O 3 X 7 % Q

Q14. Which among the following letter is 3rd to the right of the 12th element from the left end in the given series?
explanation: The 12 th element is S. Looking at the letters to the right of S: J (1 st), M (2 nd), L (3 rd). Thus L is the 3 rd letter to the right.

q:

Q17. If all the symbols and consonants are dropped from the above given series then which element is 7th from the right end?

q:
Directions $(14-18)$: Study the following alphanumeric symbol series carefully and answer the questions given below:
F 5 * E ^ 2 D U # Z R S & 8 9 @ J M 1 L O 3 X 7 % Q

Q18. How many alphabets are there between (according to English Alphabetical Order) of the letter which is 4th from the right end and the letter which is 6th from the left end?
explanation: $Letter 4 th from right is L. Letter 6 th from left is R. Letters between L and R in alphabet is 18-12 - 1 = 5$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In the number ’27486935’, how many pairs of digits have the same number between them (both forward and backward direction) in the number series?
q: In the number ’27486935’, how many pairs of digits have the same number between them (both forward and backward direction) in the number series?
explanation: The 3 pairs formed are: (2, 4), (2, 6), and (4, 6) (since their position differences match their value differences).

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(20-22)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below:
Point X is 8 m north of Point Y which is 4 m west of Point Z. Point Z is 7 m north of Point F. Point B is 6 m north of Point A which is 5 m east of Point F. Point E is 8 m south of Point G which is 9 m west of Point B.

Q 20. What is the total distance from Point X to Point F?
q:
Directions $(20-22)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Point X is 8m north of Point Y which is 4m west of Point Z. Point Z is 7m north of Point F. Point B is 6m north of Point A which is 5m east of Point F. Point E is 8m south of Point G which is 9m west of Point B.

Q20. What is the total distance from Point X to Point F?
explanation: $Total distance from X to F = XY + YZ + ZF = 8 + 4 + 7 = 19 m$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(20-22)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below:
Point X is 8 m north of Point Y which is 4 m west of Point Z. Point Z is 7 m north of Point F. Point B is 6 m north of Point A which is 5 m east of Point F. Point E is 8 m south of Point G which is 9 m west of Point B.

Q 21. What is the shortest distance between Point E and Point F?
q:
Directions $(20-22)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Point X is 8m north of Point Y which is 4m west of Point Z. Point Z is 7m north of Point F. Point B is 6m north of Point A which is 5m east of Point F. Point E is 8m south of Point G which is 9m west of Point B.

Q21. What is the shortest distance between Point E and Point F?
explanation: $E is at (0, 9), F is at (4, 7). Shortest distance = \sqrt((4-0)^2 + (7 - (9))^2) = \\sqrt{16 + 4} = \sqrt 20 m$.

q:

Q22. In which direction is Point Y with respect to Point B?
explanation: B is at (9, 1) and Y is at (0, 0). Y lies to the NorthWest of B.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(23-27)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below:
Seven persons—H, I, J, K, L, M, and N—appeared in the exam in different months—January, March, April, July, September, October, and November of the same year, but not necessarily in the same order.
K appeared in the month which has 31 days. K was neither the first nor the last to appear. Only two persons appeared between K and L who appeared immediately after M. The number of persons appeared before M is one less than the number of persons who appeared after N. H appeared immediately after I who appeared before J.

Q 23. How many persons appeared after M?
q:
Directions $(23-27)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Seven persons—H, I, J, K, L, M, and N—appeared in the exam in different months—January, March, April, July, September, October, and November of the same year, but not necessarily in the same order.
K appeared in the month which has 31 days. K was neither the first nor the last to appear. Only two persons appeared between K and L who appeared immediately after M. The number of persons appeared before M is one less than the number of persons who appeared after N. H appeared immediately after I who appeared before J.

Q23. How many persons appeared after M?
explanation: Chronological order of exam appearance: Jan - N, Mar - I, Apr - H, Jul - K, Sep - J, Oct - M, Nov - L. Only L appeared after M (one person).

Q 25. Which among the following statement(s) is/are true?
I. N appeared in an evennumbered month.
II. I appeared before L.
III. An even number of persons appeared before K.
q:
Directions $(23-27)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Seven persons—H, I, J, K, L, M, and N—appeared in the exam in different months—January, March, April, July, September, October, and November of the same year, but not necessarily in the same order.
K appeared in the month which has 31 days. K was neither the first nor the last to appear. Only two persons appeared between K and L who appeared immediately after M. The number of persons appeared before M is one less than the number of persons who appeared after N. H appeared immediately after I who appeared before J.

Q25. Which among the following statement(s) is/are true?
I. N appeared in an evennumbered month.
II. I appeared before L.
III. An even number of persons appeared before K.
explanation: I is false (N in Jan, which is 1 st/odd). II is true (I in Mar is before L in Nov). III is false (3 people before K, which is odd). Thus, only II is true.

Q 26. Who among the following persons appeared in October?
q:
Directions $(23-27)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Seven persons—H, I, J, K, L, M, and N—appeared in the exam in different months—January, March, April, July, September, October, and November of the same year, but not necessarily in the same order.
K appeared in the month which has 31 days. K was neither the first nor the last to appear. Only two persons appeared between K and L who appeared immediately after M. The number of persons appeared before M is one less than the number of persons who appeared after N. H appeared immediately after I who appeared before J.

Q26. Who among the following persons appeared in October?

options.text: The one who appeared immediately after N
The one who appeared two months after K

explanation: M appeared in October.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: If we form a fourletter meaningful word by using the third, fifth, sixth and eleventh letter from the left end of the word ‘DETERIORATE’, then which of the following will be the second letter of the meaningful word thus formed. If more than one such word is formed mark Z as your answer. If no meaningful word is formed, mark X as your answer?
q: If we form a fourletter meaningful word by using the third, fifth, sixth and eleventh letter from the left end of the word ‘DETERIORATE’, then which of the following will be the second letter of the meaningful word thus formed. If more than one such word is formed mark Z as your answer. If no meaningful word is formed, mark X as your answer?
explanation: The letters are T, R, I, E. The words formed are TIER, RITE, and TIRE (more than one). Hence, we mark Z.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(33-37)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below:
Six persons – A, B, C, D, E and F live in a threestorey building such that the bottommost floor is numbered as 1, the floor immediately above it is numbered as 2, and the topmost floor is numbered as 3. Each floor has two flats – Flat X and Flat Y, where Flat X is to the west of Flat Y. Flat Y of floor 2 is immediately above Flat Y of floor 1 and immediately below Flat Y of floor 3, and so on. Similarly for Flat X.
D lives on an oddnumbered floor. E lives immediately above D but in a different named flat. As many floors are above D as below F. D and F live in different named flat. A lives just northwest of C.

Q 33. Which of the following combinations is true?
q:
Directions $(33-37)$: Study the following information carefully and answer the questions given below:
Six persons – A, B, C, D, E and F live in a threestorey building such that the bottommost floor is numbered as 1, the floor immediately above it is numbered as 2, and the topmost floor is numbered as 3. Each floor has two flats – Flat X and Flat Y, where Flat X is to the west of Flat Y. Flat Y of floor 2 is immediately above Flat Y of floor 1 and immediately below Flat Y of floor 3, and so on. Similarly for Flat X.
D lives on an oddnumbered floor. E lives immediately above D but in a different named flat. As many floors are above D as below F. D and F live in different named flat. A lives just northwest of C.

Q33. Which of the following combinations is true?
explanation:
Floor layout:
- $Floor 3: Flat X = B, Flat Y = F$
- Floor 2: Flat X = A, Flat Y = E
- $Floor 1: Flat X = D, Flat Y = C$
Hence, 'D - Floor 1, Flat X' is true.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(38-40)$: In these question, relationship between different elements is shown in the statements. These statements are followed by two conclusions. Give answer

Q 38. $Statements: J ≤ K < L = M > N ≥ O$
Conclusions:
I. K < N
II. J < M
q:
Directions $(38-40)$: In these questions, relationship between different elements is shown in the statements. These statements are followed by two conclusions. Give answer

Q38. $Statements: J ≤ K < L = M > N ≥ O$
Conclusions:
I. K < N
II. J < M

options.text: If only conclusion I is true
If only conclusion II is true
If either conclusion I or II is true
If neither conclusion I nor II is true
If both conclusions I and II are true

explanation: $I: Relationship between K and N cannot be established since opposite signs (K < L = M > N). II: J ≤ K < L = M implies J < M (True). Only II follows$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
Directions $(38-40)$: In these question, relationship between different elements is shown in the statements. These statements are followed by two conclusions. Give answer

Q 39. $Statements: P = Q ≤ R < S = T ≥ U$
Conclusions:
I. Q < T
II. S $\ge$ U
q:
Directions $(38-40)$: In these questions, relationship between different elements is shown in the statements. These statements are followed by two conclusions. Give answer

Q39. $Statements: P = Q ≤ R < S = T ≥ U$
Conclusions:
I. Q < T
II. S ≥ U

options.text: If only conclusion I is true
If only conclusion II is true
If either conclusion I or II is true
If neither conclusion I nor II is true
If both conclusions I and II are true

explanation: I: Q $\le$ R < S = T implies Q < T (True). II: S = T $\ge$ U implies S $\ge$ U (True). Both follow.

II. X ≥ B

options.text: If only conclusion I is true
If only conclusion II is true
If either conclusion I or II is true
If neither conclusion I nor II is true
If both conclusions I and II are true

explanation: Opposite signs are present between X and A/B, so no relationship can be established. Neither follows.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The perimeter of a square is half of the perimeter of a rectangle. If side of square is 16 cm and breadth of rectangle is 20 cm, then find the length of the rectangle (in cm)?
q: The perimeter of a square is half of the perimeter of a rectangle. If side of square is 16 cm and breadth of rectangle is 20 cm, then find the length of the rectangle (in cm)?
explanation: $Perimeter \text{ of } square = 16 * 4 = 64 cm. Perimeter \text{ of } rectangle = 64 * 2 = 128 cm ⇒ 2(L + 20) = 128 ⇒ L = 44 cm$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A shopkeeper sold an article for Rs 3000 after allowing a discount of 20%. If shopkeeper made a profit of 20%, then find the difference between marked price and cost price of the article (in Rs)?
q: A shopkeeper sold an article for Rs 3000 after allowing a discount of 20%. If shopkeeper made a profit of 20%, then find the difference between marked price and cost price of the article (in Rs)?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The average weight of a group of 30 people is 72.5 kg. Two people having total weight of 181 kg left the group and three new people having total weight of 393 kg joined the group. Find the resultant average weight of the group (in kg)?
q: The average weight of a group of 30 people is 72.5 kg. Two people having total weight of 181 kg left the group and three new people having total weight of 393 kg joined the group. Find the resultant average weight of the group (in kg)?
explanation: $Initial total sum = 30 * 72.5 = 2175 kg. New sum = 2175-181 + 393 = 2387 kg. New total people = 31. Resultant average = 2387 / 31 = 77 kg$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The still water speed of a boat is 10 km/hr and the boat covers 39 km downstream in three hours. Find the total time taken by the boat to cover 26 km downstream and 14 km upstream (in hours)?
q: The still water speed of a boat is 10 km/hr and the boat covers 39 km downstream in three hours. Find the total time taken by the boat to cover 26 km downstream and 14 km upstream (in hours)?
explanation: $Downstream speed = 39 / 3 = 13 km/h. Speed \text{ of } stream = 13-10 = 3 km/h. Upstream speed = 10-3 = 7 km/h. Time taken = 26/13 + 14/7 = 2 + 2 = 4 hours$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Ram and Rahul invested Rs 4500 & Rs 1800 in a business. After six months, Ram left the business. Find the profit share ratio of Ram to Rahul at the end of the year?
q: Ram and Rahul invested Rs 4500 & Rs 1800 in a business. After six months, Ram left the business. Find the profit share ratio of Ram to Rahul at the end of the year?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The length of train A is 150 meters and it cross a 250 meters long platform in 16 seconds. If speed of train B is 40% more than that of train A, then find the speed of train B (in meters/seconds)?
q: The length of train A is 150 meters and it cross a 250 meters long platform in 16 seconds. If speed of train B is 40% more than that of train A, then find the speed of train B (in meters/seconds)?
explanation: $Speed \text{ of } A = (150 + 250)/16 = 25 m/s. Speed \text{ of } B = 25 * 1.4 = 35 m/s$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A man spends 25% of his income on house rent. From the remaining income, he spends 80% on food and grocery. If his saving is Rs 6000, then find the amount spent on house rent (in Rs)?
q: A man spends 25% of his income on house rent. From the remaining income, he spends 80% on food and grocery. If his saving is Rs 6000, then find the amount spent on house rent (in Rs)?
explanation: $Let Income = 100 x. Rent = 25 x. Remaining = 75 x. Food = 80\% \text{ of } 75 x = 60 x. Savings = 75 x - 60 x = 15 x. Since 15 x = 6000 ⇒ x = 400. Rent = 25 x = Rs 10,000$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A contains mixture of milk and water in the ratio $5:3$ respectively. If 8 liters of the mixture is taken out and replaced with water, then milk and water becomes equal in resultant mixture. Find the initial quantity of mixture?
q: A contains mixture of milk and water in the ratio $5:3$ respectively. If 8 liters of the mixture is taken out and replaced with water, then milk and water becomes equal in resultant mixture. Find the initial quantity of mixture?
explanation: $Let mixture = 8 x. Resultant Milk = 5 x - 5. Resultant Water = 3 x - 3 + 8 = 3 x + 5. Equal: 5 x - 5 = 3 x + 5 ⇒ 2 x = 10 ⇒ x = 5. Initial mixture = 8 x = 40 L$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Abhi takes loan of Rs. 25000 and repays an amount of Rs. 31,000 at the end of 2 years. What is the rate of simple interest at which he repays the loan?
q: Abhi takes loan of Rs. 25000 and repays an amount of Rs. 31,000 at the end of 2 years. What is the rate of simple interest at which he repays the loan?

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Ram and Shyam together can do a work in 16 days. Shyam takes twice time as Ram takes to do the same work. In how many days Shyam alone can complete that work?
q: Ram and Shyam together can do a work in 16 days. Shyam takes twice time as Ram takes to do the same work. In how many days Shyam alone can complete that work?
explanation: $Efficiency Ram : Shyam = 2:1. Total work = (2 + 1) * 16 = 48 units. Shyam alone takes 48 / 1 = 48 days$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
explanation: $Pattern: term * 2 + 2 = next term. 8 * 2 + 2 = 18. So 16 is wrong$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
explanation: $Pattern \text{ of } differences: +18, +21, +24, +27, +30, +33. Next term = 48 + 18 = 66, so 64 is wrong$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
explanation: $Pattern \text{ of } differences: +5, +10, +15, +20, +25, +30. Thus, 55 + 25 = 80, so 78 is wrong$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
explanation: $Pattern \text{ of } differences: 23, 21, 19, 17, 15, 13 (subtracting odd numbers). Thus 147-23 = 124, so 120 is wrong$.

q: Directions $(56-60)$: The bar graph given below shows number of Q59. If selling price of each laptop sold in 2010 is Rs 400 and each laptop sold at loss of 20%, then find total cost price of all laptops sold in 2010?
explanation: $CP \text{ of } each laptop = 400 / 0.8 = Rs 500. Total CP = 200 * 500 = Rs 100,000$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Directions $(61-65)$: The table given below shows number of people participated in three exercise activities Q 61. On Sunday, total number of people participated in Yoga are 52% less than total number of people participated in Yoga and Cycling together in Monday. Find the number of people participated in Yoga in Sunday?
q: Directions $(61-65)$: The table given below shows number of people participated in three exercise activities Q61. On Sunday, total number of people participated in Yoga are 52% less than total number of people participated in Yoga and Cycling together in Monday. Find the number of people participated in Yoga in Sunday?
explanation: $Yoga + Cycling on Monday = 289 + 361 = 650. Sunday Yoga = 650 * 0.48 = 312$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Directions $(61-65)$: The table given below shows number of people participated in three exercise activities Q 62. The average number of people participated in Zumba on all three days is how many more or less than total people participated in Cycling in Tuesday?
q: Directions $(61-65)$: The table given below shows number of people participated in three exercise activities Q62. The average number of people participated in Zumba on all three days is how many more or less than total people participated in Cycling in Tuesday?
explanation: $Avg Zumba = (157 + 189 + 218)/3 = 188. Cycling on Tuesday = 227. Difference = 227-188 = 39$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: $Directions (61-65): The table given below shows number of people participated in three exercise activities Q 63. Out of total people participated in Yoga in Wednesday, the ratio of male to female 3:2. If total female participated in Cycling in Wednesday are 35 less than that of female participated in Yoga in same day, then find male participated in Cycling in Wednesday?$
q: $Directions (61-65): The table given below shows number of people participated in three exercise activities Q63. Out of total people participated in Yoga in Wednesday, the ratio of male to female 3:2. If total female participated in Cycling in Wednesday are 35 less than that of female participated in Yoga in same day, then find male participated in Cycling in Wednesday?$
explanation: $Female Yoga Wed = 245 * 2/5 = 98. Female Cycling Wed = 98-35 = 63. Male Cycling Wed = 167-63 = 104$.

q: Directions $(61-65)$: The table given below shows number of people participated in three exercise activities Q65. Find the difference between total people participated in Zumba in Wednesday and total people participated in Yoga in Monday?
explanation: $Zumba Wed = 218. Yoga Mon = 289. Difference = 289-218 = 71$.
